---
title: ""
about:
  template: trestles
  image: Castro_picture.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: email
      href: mailto:francisca.castro@uc3m.es
    - icon: github
      text: github
      href: https://github.com/frcastrog
    - icon: cloud
      text: bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/fcastrog.bsky.social
---

My name is Francisca Castro and I'm a visiting professor in political science at Universidad Carlos III de Madrid. 

Previously, I was a postdoctoral researcher at the International Security and Development Center ([ISDC](https://isdc.org/)) in Berlin. 

I'm also associated to the ERC project [Participation & Representation in the Digital Age](https://www.prd-erc.eu/).

I obtained my PhD in Political Science from Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin, where I worked as a Doctoral Researcher in the Chair of International Politics.

My research interests are intrastate conflict, protests and social movements, state repression, democratic backsliding, and authoritarian legacies.

You can contact me at **francisca.castro@uc3m.es**

---

**News & Updates**

- Between June 10-12, we'll host the inaugural conference of the [Instituto Juan Linz](https://ijlinz.es/), where I'll present joint work with [Winnie Xia](https://www.winniexia.com/) on the killing of environmental defenders and support for climate action.  

- On June the 5th, I'll participate in the workshop Elections & Political Parties in Africa and Latin America, organized by the University of Reading, where I'll present work in progress on how memory sites affect far-right support in Chile. You can find the paper [here](https://francisca-castro.com/research/working-papers/far-right/Memory_sites_and_far_right_vote.pdf).

- Our paper **The Mobilizing Potential of Mass Emigration: Experimental Evidence from Honduras** was accepted for publication at the Journal of Politics. You can read the accepted version [here](https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/10.1086/742109).
